items.title: No flight disruptions yet despite soaring jet fuel costs, says Loke
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: No flight disruptions have been reported so far, despite mounting cost pressures on Malaysia's aviation sector from surging jet fuel prices, Transport Minister Anthony Loke said today. Loke said there is no government policy to control air ticket prices, except for limited interventions during festive seasons which involve subsidies. He added that air travel operates in a free market, with prices determined by supply and demand rather than government-imposed caps.

items.title: Illegal cigarettes hit sundry shop sales
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Sundry shops are losing customers to illegal cigarette sellers that remain widely available, according to the Federation of Sundry Goods Merchants Associations of Malaysia. Its president Hong Chee Meng said the continued presence of illicit cigarettes is placing increasing pressure on legitimate neighbourhood retailers. Hong said the wide price gap gives illegal sellers a strong advantage.

items.title: Keeping engines running: Govt says Budi Diesel aid to continue amid volatile fuel market
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 31 — The Anwar government will maintain the Budi Diesel aid at RM300 per month to help Malaysians cope with rising global fuel prices. The Finance Ministry announced today that an additional interim RM100 will continue, keeping total support for individuals and recipients under the Budi Agri‑Commodity scheme at RM300 each. “This step is being implemented by the government to ensure that diesel users, especially individuals and the agri-commodity sector, continue to receive support and are protected from being fully exposed to global fuel price increases,” the ministry said in a press statement.

items.title: 3 photographers charged for not possessing identification documents
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Three "freelance photographers" were charged in court today for lacking valid identification documents. Dang Wangi police chief, Assistant Commissioner Sazalee Adam, said: "The three individuals were charged in court today. The three photographers were also found carrying fake MyKad.

items.title: Malaysia resilient as MTEN studies global energy crisis, Anwar vows proactive measures, welfare focus
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 31 — The National Economic Action Council (MTEN) today conducted an in depth review of the escalating global energy crisis and its implications for Malaysia’s economy and the well being of the people. We must remain disciplined, resilient and united,” he said in a Facebook post. Acknowledging the scale of the challenge, Anwar emphasised that Malaysia’s economic fundamentals remain strong and resilient.

items.title: Six injured in Kajang-Semenyih crash, police nab SUV driver for alleged drink-driving
items.summary: Kajang district police chief ACP Naazron Abdul Yusof said the accident occurred at about 1.20 am involving a Range Rover Velar sports utility vehicle (SUV) driven by a 51-year-old man and a Mazda van. “The accident occurred when an SUV on the Kajang - Semenyih Bypass suddenly lost control and hit the back of a van, causing the vehicle to overturn onto the left side of the road. “As a result of the accident, the van driver and five passengers aged seven to 51 suffered injuries and are being treated at the Kajang Hospital and Serdang Hospital while the SUV driver was not injured,” he said in a statement today.

items.title: Energy security remains paramount amid escalating geopolitical tensions: Petronas
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Energy security will remain paramount as the global energy system comes under increasing strain from escalating geopolitical tensions in West Asia, Petroliam Nasional Bhd (Petronas) said today. Its president and group chief executive officer Tan Sri Tengku Muhammad Taufik Tengku Aziz said if the entirely avoidable confrontation in the region unfolds in the worst possible way, many countries would directly feel the absence of energy security, if they have not already begun experiencing its impact. Tengku Muhammad Taufik said these developments underscored an uncomfortable dilemma, where efforts to pursue other priorities cannot come at the expense of energy security.

items.title: Police arrest 25 touting photography services at KLCC and Saloma Link Bridge
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Police detained 25 individuals, including a Filipino national for allegedly touting photography services to foreign tourists near the Petronas Twin Towers and the Saloma Link Bridge yesterday. Dang Wangi police chief Assistant Commissioner Sazalee Adam said investigations found that the group operated in tourist hotspots such as KLCC and the Saloma Link, targeting visitors with paid photography services. "All those arrested, aged between 16 and 48, comprise 24 locals, including one woman, and one Filipino national," he told reporters at the Dang Wangi police headquarters today.

items.title: Govt keeps Budi Diesel at RM300 for April
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The government will maintain the Budi Madani Diesel (Budi Diesel) cash assistance at RM300 per month for April, as it seeks to cushion the impact of rising global fuel prices on targeted groups. The Finance Ministry said the additional interim RM100 assistance introduced last month will be continued, keeping total monthly aid for Budi Individu and Budi Agri-Komoditi recipients at RM300. Budi Individu provides monthly cash assistance to eligible diesel vehicle owners, while Budi Agri-Komoditi supports small-scale farmers, livestock breeders and smallholders registered with relevant ministries.

items.title: No disruptions yet, but aviation and shipping sectors under strain, says transport minister
items.summary: He said the government is closely monitoring the situation and has been engaging regularly with key industry players, particularly airlines, as challenges intensify. “Airlines are experiencing a significant increase in fuel costs, especially jet fuel. “The shipping sector is facing similar challenges.

items.title: Klang crash raises question: Can victims claim if driver was drunk or on drugs?
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Victims of road accidents involving drunk or drug-impaired drivers in Malaysia are still entitled to compensation, despite widespread misconceptions that such claims are automatically void, legal experts said. Lawyer Datuk Shanmugam Ganesan told the New Straits Times that Malaysian insurance law is fundamentally designed to protect third-party victims — not errant drivers. "Many people assume that if a driver is intoxicated, any insurance claim becomes invalid.

items.title: Loke: Iranian government assures safe passage, no tolls for Malaysian ships in Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: KLANG, March 31 — Transport Minister Anthony Loke said Malaysia has received assurances from Iran that its vessels will be able to pass safely through the Strait of Hormuz, following concerns raised over recent developments in the region. He also clarified that Malaysian vessels will not be subject to any tolls when passing through the strait. On Saturday, The Star reported that the seven Malaysian vessels currently held up in the Strait of Hormuz are expected to resume their journey soon after Malaysia’s diplomatic efforts with Iran.

items.title: Tune Talk targets 5mil subscribers ahead of IPO plans
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Tune Talk Sdn Bhd is targeting five million subscribers before pursuing an initial public offering (IPO), its co-founder and chief executive officer Gurtaj Singh Padda said. He said the company currently has 1.8 million subscribers and aims to approach the five million mark by end-2027. In February, Tune Talk established an Employee Share Trust Scheme to recognise employee contributions and position staff as equity participants.

items.title: E‑wallet operator TNG Digital introduces employee fuel subsidy scheme from April 1
items.summary: PETALING JAYA, March 31 — TNG Digital Sdn Bhd will roll out an additional RON95 petrol subsidy for its employees from April 1, supplementing the Budi95 scheme once their allocated quota is used up. The e-wallet operator said it would be the first private employer in Malaysia to introduce such a measure, aimed at cushioning workers from fluctuations in fuel prices, Free Malaysia Today (FMT) reported. It extends to taking care of the people who build those products every day,” said TNG Digital CEO Alan Ni in a statement.

items.title: Over 10,000 fake royal accounts taken down - Fahmi
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Over 10,000 fake accounts reportedly misusing the identities of His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, Her Majesty Raja Permaisuri Agong Raja Zarith Sofiah, Queen of Malaysia and the royal family for fraud and scams have been taken down. Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said the figure also includes accounts impersonating members of the Pahang royal family. MORE TO COME © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: BAT Malaysia to trim workforce ahead of distribution revamp
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: British American Tobacco (Malaysia) Bhd (BAT Malaysia) will undertake a workforce optimisation exercise as part of efforts to improve operational efficiency. "The workforce optimisation is part of the company's operational efficiency initiative and is intended to align its workforce with future operating requirements and business direction," it said. BAT Malaysia said affected employees will receive their statutory and contractual entitlements, including retrenchment benefits where applicable.

items.title: NetApp appoints Moti Uttam as Malaysia country manager
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Nasdaq-listed NetApp Inc's unit NetApp Malaysia Sdn Bhd has appointed Moti Uttam as country manager, as the company strengthens its local leadership. The company said the appointment will support its engagement with Malaysian enterprises as they modernise data infrastructure and adopt artificial intelligence (AI). NetApp Asean senior director Angeline Lim said the appointment reflects the company's focus on supporting enterprise adoption of data and AI capabilities.

items.title: Loke: 7 Malaysian ships cleared for toll-free passage through Hormuz
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: Transport Minister Anthony Loke today confirmed that seven Malaysian-owned vessels currently stranded at the Strait of Hormuz will soon be allowed to pass through toll-free. Loke said Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim had conveyed the development to the National Economic Action Council (NEAC), following Teheran's commitment to grant passage in view of Malaysia's close diplomatic ties with Iran. He also said the vessels would not be subject to any toll charges and would be allowed to transit the strait free of charge .

items.title: King calls for prudent public spending amid global uncertainty
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, has highlighted the importance of prudent public spending to safeguard national resources and ensure they are managed effectively and efficiently. Also present were National Security Council director-general Datuk Raja Nushirwan Zainal Abidin and Petronas president and group chief executive officer Tan Sri Tengku Muhammad Taufik Tengku Kamadjaja Aziz. The briefing also covered national security matters, such as internal security, border control, and the preparedness of security forces.

items.title: BYD not singled out, Miti says CKD rules designed to protect vendor ecosystem and boost exports
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 31 — The Ministry of Investment, Trade and Industry (Miti) said it imposes no isolated unfavourable conditions on carmakers, stressing that the requirements reflect a consistent policy applied to all new automotive investments in Malaysia since September 2025. This follows reports that Chinese electric vehicle (EV) manufacturer BYD is re-evaluating its plans for a local completely knocked down (CKD) assembly plant in Tanjung Malim, Perak, claiming that the government has imposed unfavourable conditions for the carmaker. Its minister, Datuk Seri Johari Abdul Ghani, said the attached conditions are non-discriminatory and are applicable equally to all high-volume automotive assembly projects regardless of brands and countries of origin.

items.title: Rawang temple row: Yayasan Kubra wins bid to quash court order over land ownership inquiry
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: Yayasan Kubra succeeded in its application at the High Court today to set aside the preliminary order of the Selayang magistrate's court. Judicial Commissioner Suriyati Hasimah Mohd Hashim made the decision after hearing arguments from lawyer Muhammad Hariz Md Yusoff, who represented the foundation as applicant, while lawyer Rajesh Nagarajan, who represented the Sri Uchimalai Muniswaraman Devotees Association, as respondent. "The court allows the applicant's application and therefore, the decision of the Selayang magistrate's court is set aside.

items.title: Malaysia open to China automotive investments, says Johari
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia remains open to automotive investments from China, with its policy framework designed to facilitate high-value participation rather than restrict market entry, the Investment, Trade and Industry Ministry (Miti) said. He said BYD Automotive Malaysia Sdn Bhd was granted an interim manufacturing licence on Sept 29, 2025, while Chery Automobile Co Ltd received approval on June 26, 2025 to undertake local vehicle production. Johari said Malaysia's automotive policies are developmental in nature, aimed at moving the country towards advanced manufacturing rather than limiting competition.

items.title: Teachers 'relieved' after first SPM cohort without UPSR, PT3 shines
items.summary: BANGI: Teachers felt "relieved and grateful" as last year's Sijil Pelajaran Malaysia (SPM) results were surprisingly strong, marking the first batch without Ujian Pencapaian Sekolah Rendah and Pentaksiran Tingkatan 3. Computer Science teacher Norzila Oon described an emotional and rewarding day as students received their SPM results, with her department achieving a 100 per cent pass rate. Teacher Suzara Makpol from Sekolah Menengah Kebangsaan Dato Abu Bakar Baginda said the students' outstanding SPM results were especially meaningful given the challenges of the academic year.

items.title: Bank Negara: Fuel subsidy changes unlikely to drive broad inflation
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Bank Negara Malaysia expects fuel subsidy rationalisation and supply chain costs to have a limited impact on inflation, without triggering broad-based price increases. Governor Datuk Seri Abdul Rasheed Ghaffour said Malaysia's inflation outlook of 1.5 per cent to 2.5 per cent reflects expectations that external cost pressures will remain contained, supported by stable domestic demand and moderate cost conditions. "Demand conditions are also important, as higher prices can trigger adjustments in demand," Abdul Rasheed said.

items.title: Malaysia should leverage Japan ties to win investment, says Nazir
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia should capitalise on its political stability and long-standing ties with Japan to attract foreign investment, particularly as global uncertainties, including the Middle East conflict, continue to disrupt supply chains. Asean Business Advisory Council Malaysia chairman Tan Sri Nazir Razak said the country has maintained a strong track record in foreign direct investment, remaining an attractive destination despite ongoing global volatility. "This is yet another opportunity for Malaysia.

items.title: Education Ministry to develop long-term plan to face heatwaves
items.summary: KAJANG: The Education Ministry will develop a comprehensive long-term plan to face heatwaves to minimise disruptions to the academic calendar and to protect students' health. "We get guidance and advice from the Health Ministry and Malaysian Meteorological Department. Fadhlina said the ministry's top priority was the safety and well-being of the school community.

items.title: Miti reviewing CBU floor price, says no ban on new pickups
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Investment, Trade and Industry Ministry (Miti) is reviewing the minimum price policy for imported vehicles as part of efforts to strengthen local automotive assembly while allowing market forces to play a greater role. Minister Datuk Seri Johari Abdul Ghani said the current policy sets a minimum on-the-road (OTR) price of RM250,000 for completely built-up (CBU) passenger vehicles under the Approved Permit (AP) framework. "Miti is currently reviewing the 'floor price' policy to further strengthen local assembly activities while ensuring that CBU vehicle pricing is driven by market forces," he said in a statement, in response to a news report on conditions imposed on Chinese EV maker BYD.

items.title: Students turn to YouTube, self-study to ace SPM
items.summary: BANGI: Watching YouTube was a key learning tool for some students who sat for the Sijil Pelajaran Malaysia (SPM) examination as the first batch without taking PT3 and UPSR, with their approach shaped by online learning during the Covid-19 pandemic. Some students from SMK Dato Abu Bakar Baginda in Sungai Merab, Bandar Baru Bangi, turned to YouTube as a primary learning tool for weaker subjects since Form Two. "I started learning through YouTube from Form Two.

items.title: SPM 2025 success: Multi-level interventions drive best results in 5 years
items.summary: Education Minister Fadhlina Sidek attributed the 2025 cohort's outstanding results to a robust "education ecosystem" shaped through ongoing performance dialogues and comprehensive support frameworks. "At the level of state education departments, district education offices, and schools, we have implemented an accountability approach for principals to ensure attendance and manage student support systems," she said. Highlighting that financial hardship remained a key barrier to education, Fadhlina pledged that the MoE would continue providing targeted socio-economic support to keep underprivileged students in school.

items.title: [UPDATED] Appellate court to decide on federal bid to stay Sabah's 40pct revenue claim
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: The Court of Appeal will rule on April 6 on the federal government's bid to stay the execution of Sabah's 40 per cent revenue entitlement. Sabah Law Society (SLS) president Datuk Mohamed Nazim Maduarin said the Court of Appeal today concluded the hearing, which was conducted via Zoom. Mohamed Nazim said the SLS will consider the Court of Appeal's decision once delivered.

items.title: Selangor government preparing WFH plan for civil servants as global oil prices fluctuate, says MB
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, March 31 — The Selangor state government is finalising a work-from-home (WFH) plan at the state administration level to help reduce fuel consumption, said Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari. He said the initiative is also expected to improve work flexibility and conserve energy, particularly amid fluctuating global oil prices caused by the ongoing crisis in West Asia. “The Selangor human resource division is preparing the plan.

items.title: Cancer patient Fernandez Minggat makes SMK Tinggi Kajang proud [WATCH]
items.summary: KAJANG: For Fernandez Minggat, the journey to the Sijil Pelajaran Malaysia (SPM) was not marked by classroom lessons and library sessions, but with hospital stays and gruelling cancer treatments. Diagnosed with cancer and paralysed from the waist down, Fernandez became a symbol of grit for SMK Tinggi Kajang, proving that even the most daunting medical battles cannot extinguish the flame of ambition. "I've been hospitalised too many times to count," he said, reflecting on the long months of recovery.

items.title: 9A SPM student inspired by parents' hard work
items.summary: IPOH: Determined to secure a brighter future for her family, Wan Aleya Batrisyia Meor Roslan achieved an impressive 9A in the Sijil Pelajaran Malaysia (SPM) examination. I just wanted to make my parents proud," she said when met at the school today. Wan Aleya is one of 13 students at the school to score straight As.

items.title: SPM 2025: Student with spinal bifida scores 10As
items.summary: ALOR SETAR: Despite the challenges of spinal bifida, Huda Rasheeda Rosli, a student at Sekolah Menengah Agama (SMA) Darusaadah here, has demonstrated extraordinary determination, achieving 10As in the 2025 Sijil Pelajaran Malaysia (SPM). She is one of three special educational needs candidates in Kedah to achieve outstanding results, alongside Yousef Mohd Hazril Munir from Sekolah Menengah Kebangsaan (SMK) Khir Johari and Nur Zahra Arifah Abd Rahman from SMA Islahiah Mahang. Huda Rasheeda said she had to stay in the school hostel for several days before being allowed to return home and continue her examinations at her original school once the floodwaters receded.

items.title: Palestinians continue to struggle for justice
items.summary: ON the 50th anniversary of Palestinian Land Day (March 30, 2026), we stand united in resistance. Since 1976, Israel has occupied our land (West Bank, Gaza and East Jerusalem). In Gaza, Israel has killed over 70,000 Palestinians since Oct 7, 2023.

items.title: Fadhlina: First batch without UPSR, PT3 excels in 2025 SPM
items.summary: KAJANG: Education Minister Fadhlina Sidek said the 2025 Sijil Pelajaran Malaysia (SPM) cohort, the first to sit without Ujian Pencapaian Sekolah Rendah (UPSR) and Pentaksiran Tingkatan 3 (PT3), has shown strong resilience. She said the batch's performance contributed to SPM 2025 recording its best results in five years. Fadhlina said the absenteeism rate fell to 1.42 per cent in 2025 from 1.81 per cent in the previous year.

items.title: Lim Guan Eng presses for urgent aid to MSMEs facing rising costs from Middle East turmoil
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 31 — Former finance minister Lim Guan Eng has urged the government to introduce immediate financial relief measures for micro, small and medium enterprises (MSMEs) facing rising operational costs linked to the Middle East conflict. He said escalating logistics expenses, volatile raw material prices and supply chain instability, compounded by concerns over oil prices, were threatening the survival of Malaysian businesses. “The time is now for the Malaysian government to act immediately to implement financial relief policies for Malaysian businesses, especially micro small and medium enterprises (MSMEs), to save them from a crisis of survival impacted by the escalating costs of the Middle-East war,” Lim said in a statement today.

items.title: Clerk's son tops SPM with 10A+ as Penang maintains steady performance
items.summary: Sanjay, a Pure Science stream student who also took Principles of Accounting, said his success was rooted in constant practice and paying close attention in class. To strengthen his understanding, he attended tuition classes for six subjects, including Science, Additional Mathematics and Accounting. The Accounts stream student, who obtained A+ in Additional Mathematics, Principles of Accounting, English and Mathematics, said attending extra classes at school was crucial.

items.title: Man escapes noose, gets 35 years, 12 strokes for killing neighbour
items.summary: Judge Norliza Othman also sentenced S. Panirselvam, 42, to serve 15 years for seriously injuring a woman in the same incident. "The court also finds that, although you were called to enter defence, you failed to raise any reasonable doubt to rebut the prosecution's case," she said. The charge under Section 302 carries the death penalty or a jail term between 30 and 40 years upon conviction.

items.title: High Court finds Najib liable for RM4.77b SRC losses, orders RM5.25b payment
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 31 — The High Court here today ruled that Datuk Seri Najib Tun Razak is liable for SRC International Sdn Bhd’s US$1.3 billion (RM5.25 billion) losses after finding that he had breached his fiduciary duties, abused his position, and misappropriated the company’s funds for personal gain. Judge Datuk Ahmad Fairuz Zainol Abidin, in allowing the company’s US$1.18 billion (RM4.77 billion) lawsuit against the former premier, ordered Najib to pay the amount, in addition to US$120 million that he had received from the company into his AmPrivate banking account ending 964, hence bringing the total to US$1.3 billion. Justice Ahmad Fairuz said the court further observed that but for Najib’s misuse of office, SRC would not have borrowed RM4 billion on ineligible grounds, dispersed RM3.6 billion offshore through fraudulent structures, or lost US$1.2 billion in misappropriated funds.

items.title: Perak KPDN raids Teluk Intan premises, detains Bangladeshi, seizes 450 subsidised cooking oil packets
items.summary: IPOH, March 31 — The Perak Enforcement Division of the Ministry of Domestic Trade and Cost of Living (KPDN) seized 450 packets of 1kg subsidised cooking oil from a residential premises believed to have been used as an illegal storage facility in Teluk Intan. “Enforcement officers raided a residential premises in Teluk Intan which was suspected of being used as an illegal storage facility for controlled goods, namely packet cooking oil. “During the inspection, we discovered 450 packets of 1kg subsidised cooking oil of various brands stored at the premises,” he said in a statement.

items.title: Murder charge for Singaporean in JB court over death of fellow citizen
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, March 31 — A 25-year-old Singaporean man was charged in the Magistrate’s Court here today for the murder of a fellow countryman in Southkey here last month. The accused, Muhammad Arif Saiyadi, understood the charge read out by the court interpreter, however no plea was recorded before Magistrate Nabilah Nizam. According to the charge sheet, the man was charged with murder by causing the death of Chia Hon Chung, 32, at Persiaran Southkey 1, Kota Southkey here at 5.30am on March 14.

items.title: Pontian municipal officer pleads not guilty to cheating charges involving RM62,760 over building application
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, March 31 — A 38 year old municipal administrator claimed trial in the Sessions Court here today on a charge of deceiving a man into handing over a cheque and cash totalling RM62,760 to speed up the application for the construction of a two storey building in Rimba Terjun, Pontian, over the past five years. The accused, Muhammad Nazri Abu Bakar, who is attached to the Pontian Municipal Council (MPPn), pleaded not guilty to five charges after they were read out by an interpreter before Judge Datuk Ahmad Kamal Arifin Ismail. Based on the first to fifth charges, the accused is charged with deceiving a 72-year-old man into handing over three cheques and cash totalling RM62,760.

items.title: Motorcyclist hurt in Yan knife attack, suspect torches bike in rage before Kedah police arrest him
items.summary: ALOR SETAR, March 31 — Police have arrested a man suspected of injuring another individual with a knife before setting the victim’s motorcycle ablaze at Jalan Singkir Badong, near Yan, yesterday. Yan Police chief Supt Mohamad Hamizi Abdullah said the incident occurred at about 7pm and involved a 34-year-old motorcyclist and a 41-year-old suspect. The suspect then removed the petrol pipe and set the motorcycle on fire,” he said in a statement today.

items.title: Knife attack at Kapar eatery leaves man wounded; police arrest suspect with prior offences
items.summary: North Klang District Police chief ACP S. Vijaya Rao said in the incident at about 6.09am, the complainant and the victim, his 69-year-old local friend, were approached by a group of men at the premises. He said the misunderstanding began when the complainant ignored remarks made by the group, which led one of the suspects to pull out a knife and swing it at the victim. Vijaya said acting on information, police arrested one of the suspects yesterday, and checks revealed that the individual had eight criminal records and three drug-related records.

items.title: Penang DOSH clamps down on Bukit Minyak site after worker killed by falling plywood from crane
items.summary: BUKIT MERTAJAM, March 31 — The Department of Occupational Safety and Health (DOSH) Penang has issued a Prohibition Notice and a stop-work order to the employer at a construction site in the Bukit Minyak Industrial Park following a fatal accident recently. He said the foreign worker later died at Hospital Seberang Jaya on March 24 due to severe injuries sustained in the fall. “The accident occurred at the construction site on March 19.

items.title: BNM says new Consumer Credit Commission will oversee BNPL sector amid rising debt and rapid expansion
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 31 — The establishment of the Consumer Credit Commission is timely and important in safeguarding consumers through the regulation and supervision of previously unregulated nonbank credit and credit service providers, including buy now pay later (BNPL) providers. “The establishment of the commission will bring previously unregulated non-bank credit providers and credit service providers, including BNPL providers, under a formal regulatory and supervisory framework. “Correspondingly, total outstanding BNPL debt increased to RM4.9 billion as of end- December 2025 from RM3.8 billion in June 2025.

items.title: SPM 2025 levels up as results hit record high, more candidates earn certificates, grade average improves
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, March 31 — The 2025 Sijil Pelajaran Malaysia (SPM) examination results recorded the best achievement with a National Grade Average (GPK) index of 4.42 points compared to 4.49 points in 2024. Education director-general Datuk Dr Mohd Azam Ahmad said that a total of 366,435 candidates or 94.29 per cent were eligible to be awarded the SPM certificate in 2025 compared to 355,933 candidates in 2024. “Millions of congratulations to all teachers whose efforts have been expressed in the form of SPM results,” he said in a press conference announcing the 2025 SPM results here today.

items.title: Red Honda Civic chase at KLIA ends with arrest of local man carrying 31 criminal records
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, March 31 — A police car chase in the Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) area yesterday led to the arrest of a local man with 31 prior criminal records. KLIA police chief ACP M. Ravi said the 30-year-old suspect rammed several public vehicles in an attempt to escape while being pursued by a mobile patrol vehicle (MPV) in the incident at 4.30pm. He said MPV personnel conducting crime prevention patrols around the KLIA area attempted to stop a red Honda Civic, but the driver refused to pull over for inspection.

items.title: Johor KPDN intercepts suspicious lorry, seizes 800 litres of illicit diesel worth RM20,716
items.summary: Johor KPDN director Lilis Saslinda Pornomo said in a statement that the Enforcement Patrol Unit (URP) detected a suspicious lorry and acted to detain and inspect the vehicle, at about 2pm. The IBC, she said, is believed to contain about 800 litres of diesel fuel. Lilis Saslinda said the lorry driver, in his 40s, failed to produce valid documents including a special permit for storing or purchasing diesel fuel and a statement was taken for investigation purposes.

items.title: Hannah Yeoh visits KLCC with mayor to check on unlicensed ‘photo touts’ preying on tourists
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 31 — Federal Territories Minister Hannah Yeoh last night visited the area around the Petronas Twin Towers and Suria KLCC with Kuala Lumpur mayor Datuk Seri TPr Fadlun Mak Ujud to check on the presence of unlicensed ‘photo touts’ operating at the landmark site. Yeoh said she spent more than an hour there and witnessed first-hand how the touts approached tourists to offer photography services. Authorities have pledged tighter enforcement, with Yeoh earlier announcing plans to install more closed-circuit television (CCTV) cameras and increase patrols in the area.

items.title: Police confirm 10 arrests in Kulim factory trespass case, including journalist, amid tensions over migrant labour
items.summary: ALOR SETAR, March 31 — Police have confirmed the arrest of 10 men, including a portal journalist, to assist in investigations into alleged trespassing at a factory construction site in the Kulim Hi-Tech Park (KHTP), Kulim last Wednesday. Kedah police chief Datuk Adzli Abu Shah said eight of them were detained last Saturday, while two others were arrested yesterday. He said the eight individuals detained earlier have been released on police bail, while the two arrested yesterday will be brought to court today for a remand order.

items.title: With crude prices soaring, Petronas steps up monitoring and calls for smarter energy use across the nation
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 31 — Petroliam Nasional Bhd (Petronas) continues to closely monitor the nation’s fuel supply to help safeguard nationwide stability, amid the ongoing West Asia crisis. The national oil company also advises members of the public to avoid panic buying and hoarding of fuel as this would worsen the impact of this crisis. All these developments have impacted Malaysia’s fuel supply security,” it added.

items.title: Nurul Izzah shuts down viral chatter, insists she’s firmly holding on to PKR deputy president role
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 31 — Nurul Izzah Anwar has denied claims that she has stepped down as PKR deputy president. Speculation that Nurul Izzah had vacated the party’s deputy president’s post went viral on social media recently, sparking various reactions within the party. The daughter of PKR president Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim was elected as the party’s deputy president for the 2025-2028 term in last year’s elections, defeating incumbent Datuk Seri Mohd Rafizi Ramli in a straight fight.

items.title: Include us in RM1,000 tourism tax relief to boost domestic tourism, Malaysia’s budget hotels tell govt
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 31 — The Malaysian government should encourage more Malaysians to travel locally by giving them tax relief for hotel stays, an industry group has suggested. Malaysia Budget & Business Hotel Association (MyBHA) president Sri Ganesh Michiel said this proposed tax measure would help with the expected reduced number of overseas visitors due to the Middle East conflict. — File picture by Shafwan Zaidon Minimal impact on budget hotels now Sri Ganesh, whose association represents around 2,900 hotels rated three stars and below, said the Iran war’s impact on such budget hotels “remains minimal” for now.

items.title: Give us tax relief as a ‘shortcut’ targeted aid, Malaysia’s tourism industry urges govt
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 30 — The Malaysian government can provide tax relief and tax rebates to the local inbound tourism industry, which would be faster than going through the process of providing direct subsidies for fuel costs, the Malaysian Inbound Tourism Association (MITA) said today. Under this proposed method, she proposed that the Inland Revenue Board provide tax relief or tax reductions during annual tax declarations, and said this should apply to legitimate or licensed tour agents and tourism transport operators. In the same press conference, Leong highlighted that tour buses and tour vans use diesel.

items.title: PM Anwar to chair economic council meeting on West Asia conflict tomorrow
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 30 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim is scheduled to chair the National Economic Action Council (MTEN) meeting tomorrow to discuss strategies to address the impact of the geopolitical conflict in West Asia on the country’s food security. Deputy Minister of Agriculture and Food Security Datuk Chan Foong Hin hopes the discussions will provide relief to agricultural industry players, particularly in the fisheries and crop cultivation sectors, which are currently facing major challenges due to current developments. Chan said the geopolitical conflict in West Asia is not merely a regional issue but has sparked a global chain reaction, including an energy crisis and a surge in oil prices, putting direct pressure on the economies of countries in Asia.

items.title: Transport Ministry moves to make intoxicated drivers pay compensation to victims’ families
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, March 30 — The Ministry of Transport (MOT) is drafting amendments to the Road Transport Act 1987 (Act 333) to require those convicted of driving under the influence of alcohol or drugs to pay compensation to victims’ families. Transport Minister Anthony Loke said the compensation would be in addition to prison sentences under Act 333, in line with the ministry’s uncompromising stance against drink-driving, which endangers other road users. He said under Section 44 of the Act, any individual who drives under the influence of alcohol or drugs and causes death may, upon conviction, face imprisonment of not less than 10 years and not more than 15 years, as well as a fine of between RM50,000 and RM100,000.

items.title: Selangor reopens RM1,000 higher education study grant applications until April 14
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, March 30 — The Selangor government has reopened applications for the Higher Education Institution (IPT) Study Grant until April 14 to help ease the financial burden of Selangor-born students pursuing higher education. Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari said the initiative offers a RM1,000 aid to first-year students of IPTs recognised by the state government from the Level 4 Skills Certificate to Bachelor’s Degree levels. “This year, the state government is allocating RM5 million, up from the previous RM3 million, so that more students can enjoy this benefit.

items.title: ‘Prison is not enough’: Widow vows legal action after husband killed in Klang crash involving suspected drunk driver
items.summary: Nor Nadia Abdul Majid, 32, said she will take legal action to defend the rights of their three young children, aged between two and nine, who have lost both their father and the family’s main source of income. “I will take action. I am thinking about them,” she said when met at her family home in Kampung Repoh, Batu Kurau near here yesterday.
